title: GDPO: Learning to Directly Align Language Models with Diversity Using GFlowNets

method.name: GDPO (Generative Flow Networks for Direct Preference Optimization)
method.approach: Uses GFlowNets to model preference alignment as an inference task
key_features: Learns reward signals directly from offline preference datasets
Samples from distributions proportionally to reward distributions

technical_details.terminal_state: s_f := x; y (prompt concatenated with response)

key_findings: Generates more diverse responses than baselines in dialogue generation
Generates more diverse responses than baselines in summarization tasks
Remains aligned with the preference dataset while generating diverse responses

related_work: Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) - has limitations in diversity
Regularized objectives - tend to settle around local modes
GFlowNets for tuning LLMs to induce latent chain-of-thought
problems_addressed: DPO tends to overfit on reward signals
DPO learns to reject undesired responses faster than accepting desired responses
Lack of diversity in existing methods limits applicability to creative use-cases
Under-representation of certain demographics in LM responses
